title: Daily Briefing — Jun 26
headline: June swoon is live: extreme fear, tech breakdown, and sector rotation confirm the midterm drawdown roadmap.
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us. We’re deep in the midterm-year 'June swoon' window (R521/R529), and the tape is playing by the book: Fear & Greed just tagged 24.9 (Extreme Fear, R013), tech majors are cracking the 20 MA (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, AMZN — see the price/SMA20 gap), and VIX stepped up above the 20-day. This isn’t a contrarian buy — it’s textbook seasonal weakness, with breadth thin and defensive rotation kicking in.

Sector rotation shows Industrials (XLI), Healthcare (XLV), Financials (XLF), Utilities (XLU), and Real Estate (XLRE) improving or leading — classic defensive turn as Tech (XLK) loses momentum and Energy (XLE), Materials (XLB) lag hard. Look at the SMAs: XLI, XLV, XLU, XLRE are all above their 20 and 50, while XLK and XLE break down or stagnate. The roadmap (R145/R159/R163) says Q4 rally starts AFTER this correction, not before.

Contrarian signals? Extreme fear is usually opportunity (R013), but the seasonality rules override: retail's not piling in, and dark pool flow isn’t showing mega accumulation at these levels. Bond ETFs (HYG/LQD/TLT) are holding steady — if they break, everything breaks (R001), but they're not flashing red yet. Rule triggers: 20 MA break = exit longs, don't chase, abundance mindset. Missed the move? Reposition for the next rotational play, not the fallen tech knives.
callout_body:
AAPL $275.15 (-6.1%), MSFT $352.83 (-3.5%), NVDA $195.74 (-1.6%) — all below 20 MA: bearish
SPY $734.30 below 20 MA: watch for weekly mean reversion to SMA200 ($686)
XLI $184.12, XLV $155.63, XLU $45.85 — all above SMA20/50, sector rotation play
HYG $79.88, TLT $87.35, LQD $109.50 — bond ETFs steady: no macro breakdown (yet)
Fear & Greed 24.9, VIX 18.89 > SMA20: R013/R498 triggered, not contrarian buy (midterm-year rules apply)
action_items: Exit tech longs: AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, AMZN — all triggered daily close below 20 MA
Watch for defensive rotation entries: XLV, XLU, XLI, XLRE on pullbacks to SMA20
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's in the price. Patience, react, don't predict — abundance mindset prevails.
